summary: Decompiled three functions from Toy.s in src/melee/ty/toy.c, replacing placeholder pragmas for un_80305B88, un_80305C44, and un_80305DB0 with C implementations that scan controller inputs for up to four pads and call gm_801677E8 on the active slot. Updated toy.h prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete return types and void parameter lists. Added a static inline HSD_PadGetNmlStickY accessor in controller.h after review feedback that placing the helper in controller.c shifted code and broke existing 100% matches.
what_changed: Implemented un_80305B88 as an s32-returning function that uses PAD_STACK(4), iterates pad slots 0 through 3, checks HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8)i].trigger, calls gm_801677E8(i) on the first nonzero trigger, breaks, and returns the button value.
Implemented un_80305C44 similarly, but checks HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8)i].button instead of trigger.

Implemented un_80305DB0 as a float-returning function that iterates pad slots, reads normalized stick Y through HSD_PadGetNmlStickY(i), calls gm_801677E8(i) when ABS(ret) > 0.1F, breaks, and returns the last read value.
Added includes in toy.c for mn/mnsoundtest.h, baselib/controller.h, gm/gm_1601.h, and MSL/math.h for ABS.
Changed toy.h prototypes for un_80305B88 and un_80305C44 from unknown signatures to s32 function(void), and un_80305DB0 to float function(void).
Added static inline float HSD_PadGetNmlStickY(u8 slot) in controller.h returning HSD_PadCopyStatus[slot].nml_stickY.
smart_moves: Kept the new HSD_PadGetNmlStickY helper as a static inline in controller.h instead of a real function in controller.c, avoiding insertion of non-original code into a compiled translation unit and preserving existing 100% matches.
Used controller status arrays already exposed by baselib/controller.h rather than inventing new state or abstractions.
Narrowed formerly unknown toy.h declarations to concrete signatures once the decompiled functions established return types and no-argument parameter lists.
Used PAD_STACK(4) in the button/trigger polling functions, matching the controller-related stack pattern required by these functions.
decomp_lessons: Do not add helper functions to a .c file when they did not exist in the original binary; even useful local helpers can shift code and break unrelated 100% matches.
For accessors needed only to express matched C, prefer static inline helpers in headers when similar project precedent exists, such as the reviewer-cited jobj.h inline pattern.
When decompiling controller polling loops, concrete field access to HSD_PadCopyStatus entries can clarify prototypes and return types for previously unknown functions.
Small decomp PRs can still require header hygiene: replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with precise types is part of making the recovered code reusable.
naming_conventions: Preserved unknown toy function names in address-based form: un_80305B88, un_80305C44, and un_80305DB0.
Used project-style HSD accessor naming for the inline helper: HSD_PadGetNmlStickY.
Kept global controller array naming consistent with existing baselib symbols: HSD_PadCopyStatus, HSD_PadMasterStatus, and HSD_PadGameStatus.
The PR did not introduce semantic names for the toy functions beyond their existing un_ address labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `(u8) i` casts in HSD_PadCopyStatus indexing for the trigger and button polling functions, likely to match the original code generation.
Used assignment inside the conditional, `if ((button = ...))`, so the returned button value and branch condition share the same loaded value.
Initialized `ret` to `0.0F` in un_80305DB0 and returned it after scanning, matching the no-input fallback behavior implied by the implementation.
Used `ABS(ret) > 0.1F` for normalized stick Y threshold detection.
Moved a non-original helper out of controller.c into a header inline after the build/bot indicated match breakage.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Move HSD_PadGetNmlStickY out of controller.c into controller.h, similar to inlines in jobj.h, because adding a function that does not exist in the original shifted code and broke 100% matches.

review_feedback.author: ChaseHaymond
review_feedback.feedback: Confirmed the function was moved from controller.c to an inline function in controller.h.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Noted that the Issues build had called out problems to address and suggested asking for help in Discord if needed.

follow_up_queries: Find other Toy.s placeholders near un_80305D00, un_80305EB4, un_80305FB8, and un_803060BC that may use the same controller polling patterns.
Search for other helper functions added to .c files that should instead be static inline header accessors to avoid shifting matched code.
Compare controller accessor inlines in jobj.h and controller.h for project conventions around non-original helper placement.
Audit remaining toy.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations after this PR for signatures that can be inferred from nearby decompiled functions.
